description_long: This is a modern, twin-boom airplane with a sleek, streamlined fuselage and a distinctive high-mounted straight wing configuration. The aircraft features two engines mounted on the central fuselage, with propellers positioned in a pusher arrangement behind the wings. The tail section is an H-tail design, with two vertical stabilizers connected by a horizontal stabilizer, providing stability and control. The landing gear appears to be fixed, with two main gear units under the booms and a nose gear under the forward fuselage. The cockpit is located at the front of the fuselage, enclosed with a clear canopy and designed for at least one pilot. The exterior is primarily light gray with subtle red and black accents on the wings and tail surfaces.
description: A sleek, twin-boom airplane with high-mounted straight wings, pusher propeller engines, and an H-tail design. The aircraft is light gray with red and black accents and features a forward, enclosed cockpit.
description_view_0: Front three-quarter view showing the twin-boom structure, high-mounted straight wings, and the forward cockpit canopy.
description_view_1: Rear three-quarter view highlighting the pusher propeller engines, H-tail configuration, and fixed landing gear under the booms.
description_view_2: Rear side view emphasizing the twin vertical stabilizers, horizontal stabilizer, and the engine placement behind the wings.
description_view_3: Front side view displaying the streamlined fuselage, cockpit canopy, and the overall symmetry of the wing and tail arrangement.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight wings with slight dihedral angle and no visible winglets; twin-boom structure.
attribute_engine_type: Twin pusher propeller engines mounted on the central fuselage behind the wings.
attribute_tail_design: H-tail configuration with twin vertical stabilizers connected by a horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed tricycle landing gear with two main gear units under the booms and a single nose gear.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a clear canopy, likely single or dual-seat.
